title: What You Need To Know About Pre-Production and How to Choose a Video Production Company
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
Pre-production is the process before production that helps organize and keep production running timely and on budget. Corporate videos are content designed to raise funds or explain products, using them to express ideas across digital and non-traditional platforms.

The perfect Pre-Production enables a company that is looking to generate content, a way to create a dialogue with a production company. The better your dialogue is, the more authentic and unique your company video will be.

## IDENTIFY YOUR GOALS (CREATE OUTLINE)

This foundational step requires determining the video's purpose — whether increasing brand awareness, fundraising, or expanding target audience reach. Imagine viewing the completed video start-to-finish, then document ideas without pressure before organizing them sequentially.

## CONSIDER YOUR BUDGET

Establish a comfortable spending amount, then research comparable video samples across at least three examples to understand quality variations.

### Look for Samples

Search for videos matching your objectives — such as emotional connection pieces, promotional content, or campaign launches — to establish quality benchmarks.

### Looking for a Production Company

- **Do your research:** Examine portfolios, read reviews, and seek colleague referrals from video production companies locally and online.
- **Thumbtack and Bark:** These platforms connect clients with service providers, though personal research is recommended alongside platform reviews.
- **Google:** Local searches help identify companies and assess portfolio alignment with project needs.

Upon contact, share your project outline so companies generate questions and quotes. Evaluate their responsiveness and compare estimates against your budget.

Obtain quotes from at least three companies before deciding.

- **Check their credentials:** Verify licenses, certifications, and insurance documentation.
- **Ask for a quote:** Provide comprehensive project details for accurate estimates.
- **Meet with them:** Schedule consultations to assess communication style and ask clarifying questions.
- **Choose the right fit:** Select based on cumulative research, credentials, quotes, and meetings.

### Back to Budget

Rates vary by company. If your initial budget aligns with selected vendor pricing, proceed. If quotes exceed your budget, your investment expectations require reevaluation.

## TREATMENT

A treatment presents a clear video outline organizing its purpose, varying based on video type (event, promo, etc.). The section includes three visual samples demonstrating different treatment approaches, with Sample 3 noting an accompanying promo video was produced from that treatment.

Once treatment approval occurs, most planning work is complete, allowing clients to sit back, relax, and experience the great high-quality treatment from your production team.

title: How This New Trend In Video Production Could Help You
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
The video production sector undergoes constant evolution, requiring organizations to embrace emerging technologies for sustained relevance. Currently, vertical videos is the latest trend causing leading brands to shift to a vertical format for editing and sharing videos.

Modern tools like Premiere Pro enable automatic resizing without filming vertically initially. Organizations leverage this by converting longer content into concise social media clips in vertical orientation, expanding reach and strengthening online visibility.

Vertical formatting occupies the entire mobile screen, eliminating competing visual elements and directing viewer attention. Social platforms including Stories, IGTV, and advertisements accommodate this layout seamlessly.

Research demonstrates that adverts recorded a ninety percent completion rate when presented as vertical videos, while fewer than one-third of users rotate devices for horizontal content, viewing approximately fourteen percent when they do.

However, prolonged vertical viewing becomes fatiguing — explaining why cinematic and television productions use horizontal orientation.

Compositional constraints challenge videographers, and inexperienced teams may face production delays when adopting unfamiliar formats.

Selection depends on content requirements. Short social media videos benefit from vertical framing. Lengthy content destined for websites risks unsightly black borders on traditional displays.

Since mobile consumption dominates, incorporating captions serves audiences watching without audio.

title: Top Five Things To Look For When Hiring A Video Professional
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
One of the most versatile and engaging visual mediums is video content, and it is widely used by businesses to materialize their vision and increase their online presence. So, if you're looking to take your company's brand image a notch higher by creating a compelling video, it's best to enlist the services of a team of experts that will help in producing impactful content to target your audience.

However, finding a reliable video production company that will take care of all the aspects and execute your idea seamlessly can be challenging. Also, with a slew of options to choose from, it can be taxing to pick someone that ensures a favorable outcome. To increase your knowledge and hire the right agency to fulfill your goals, here's a list of the top five things to look for in a video professional.

## 1. Experience

When looking to hire a video production company, you need to ensure that they have the expertise in producing the type of video you want for your project. An experienced production team will have the ability to demonstrate a deep range of samples across an array of styles and methods. The first thing you should do is review their portfolio. Many companies provide samples of their work on their website. These samples will give insight into the quality of video this company is willing to place their name on. While reviewing, determine if their content is attractive, engaging, and professional. Does their style match your vision? Do they seem experienced enough to understand your concept and deliver it to your standards? These are thoughts and questions you should ask yourself to ensure you engage with experienced professionals.

## 2. Open communication

A promising sign is when the right professional spends more time learning about your needs and objectives than selling themselves. The company should ask you questions to determine what exactly your recording is going to be used for and who the target audience will be. A company that is willing to have an open form or communication and listen closely to your needs will most likely deliver a video that meets and exceeds your expectations.

## 3. Company reviews

Many companies list out testimonials from clients on their website, which will all be favorable, that's why it's on their website. So, you need to also review them on Google Reviews, Thumbtack, Bark, or any other review website to get a more in-depth look at their feedback. Another way to determine if they're worth working with is to grab a few names of the companies of videos you like and ask the prospective video company if they will share their contact information with you. You will now have the opportunity to better understand their working relationship with their previous clients.

## 4. Know the work you pay for

Once you communicate your request to the video company, they should provide you with a professional and clear proposal or quote detailing what you are paying for and the time-frame for delivery. Videos will vary in price based on how many cameras, events, or services are needed for production. Some familiar service offerings are live-streaming, multiple-camera recordings, testimonial interviews, etc. Depending on the needs of the client, production companies will adjust their packages and prices accordingly. At the end of the day, make sure you ask questions if you are unclear about the services being provided to ensure you aren't getting over or under-charged.

## 5. Passion

In this creative field, passion is what drives us to keep moving throughout the day. This quality should be easy to spot the moment you connect with the video company. Does their desire to help you seem genuine? Did they offer you any helpful and creative ways to capture your vision? Ultimately, this project is your passion, and you want someone to feel as excited about it as you do. You also want to work with someone you like because that can lead to a long working relationship for future projects. Ensure you feel comfortable with your chosen video production company and confident that your video is in the right hands.

title: How to Find a Production Company: 5 Mistakes to Avoid as a Client
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
For an organized event, capturing the heart and purpose through videography can highlight the best moments. It also serves as a reminder of the special day and tends to bring back waves of memories and evoke emotions felt at that time.

Enlisting the service of a video production company will help in capturing breathtaking, memorable videos to help you share and relive experiences. Combined with their creative concepts and sophisticated professional equipment, they will produce a compelling video available to enjoy well into the future.

However, due to a lack of experience and knowledge about videographers and their services, most clients commit a few avoidable errors along the way.

Here are some of the most common mistakes to avoid when choosing to work with a production company, and the most important qualities to prioritize when you're ready to sign the contract.

## Mistake 1: Blurred Lines of Communication

Depending on the video's goal, the videographer will need to capture specific moments of the event to create a video that matches your vision. For example, we may need to capture the attendees' testimonials or shots of individual performances or presentations. If we are not at a specific area and at a particular time to record it, we will miss it.

Keep the lines of communication open and be sure to make any schedule changes known to the video team so that they can adjust their setups accordingly. It's helpful to have someone from the company working with the video team on the event day to help foster that communication.

## Mistake 2: Forgetting to Align on Set Beforehand

When you hire a video production company to record your event, you need to provide detailed information about the venue so that they can prepare the proper equipment. In most cases, the video team may visit the venue before the day of the event or arrive early enough on the day to scope out the area. This is crucial because it will provide the crew with audio and lighting information.

When informed at the earliest, all of these details can help make the process of filming your event run smoothly.

## Mistake 3: Failing to Discuss Realistic Expectations

Since recording and editing is such a timely and delicate process, it's important to set expectations and deadlines as early as possible in the process. Videographers are often capable of producing a fast turnaround if they are aware of it. Still, a quick turnaround usually means heavy workdays, so it's best to set the deadlines ahead of time.

And, on that note, expect revisions. It's unlikely that the production team will get the editing right on the first cut. Plan for enough time to review, provide revision and finalize the footage.

## Mistake 4: Failing to Inform Your Audience of Filming

The video production company you work with should be able to provide waivers for filming. This will allow any images captured to be used in the film, including brand images and attendees. That said, it's usually helpful to have a sign at the door to let people know the session is being recorded.

Another useful tip for event planners, if there are some attendees who do not wish to be on film for any reason, designate a few seats that are guaranteed to be out of the video frame and label them with a sign that says "Sit here if you do not want to be recorded."

## Mistake 5: Neglecting to Get a Copy of the Raw Footage

Your video production team should provide you with the raw footage at the end of the production day. Clients are encouraged to bring a hard drive so the video team can dump the footage on-site. (We prefer a two terabyte hard drive, which can be purchased for less than $100.)

This is helpful for two reasons:
1. You can use that footage and repurpose it as needed.
2. You simply never know what might happen.

Cameras can get stolen or damaged, so avoid the trouble and hold on to your own copy.

## What Should I Look For in a Production Company?

A strong business partnership, especially in video production, always entails:
- Fluid, open lines of communication
- A clear understanding of the set and working environment
- A strong, realistic game plan that meets your expectations and hard deadlines
- Backup strategies in the event of confusion, miscommunication or disruption of the project

When you find a production company that dots all of the i's and crosses the t's, you'll be in the right hands.

To avoid the previously listed mistakes, reach out to the experts at Transcendent Enterprise. We've been producing and crafting brilliant video content for clients for over two decades. We offer a full range of services that include corporate content creation, pre-production, 4k production, editing, live streaming, and photography and are capable of producing in various industries and fields of work.

title: Common Mistakes People Make When Hiring A Video Production Company
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
Video marketing serves as an effective tool for companies seeking to enhance brand reputation and achieve strong return on investment. It creates personality for your brand and enables connection with viewers. However, business owners often lack in-house experience and resources for video production, necessitating external professional assistance.

## Mistake 1: Hiring the lowest bidder
Price shouldn't be the sole determining factor. Gathering four to five quotes allows comparison and insight into project scope. Unusually low quotes may indicate low-quality work or misunderstanding of your vision.

## Mistake 2: Enlisting based solely on demo reels
Demo reels highlight impressive work with major brands but may not reflect your project's actual needs. Request examples of work similar to your specific project requirements.

## Mistake 3: Hiring before personal connection
Direct communication matters significantly. Assess whether the company demonstrates concern for your success, offers sensible planning, communicates effectively, and maintains friendly demeanor. Working relationship quality influences final results.

## Mistake 4: Failing to discuss timeline
Establish project deadlines before vendor selection. Confirm the company can meet your timeline through written contract. Overly busy companies risk cutting corners or causing delays.

## Mistake 5: Skimping on company role clarity
Understand what services the company provides internally versus outsourcing. Ask about storyboard development, script creation, animation capabilities, and post-production marketing distribution responsibilities.

## Mistake 6: Lack of research
Review former client testimonials and examine previous projects. Negative reviews provide clear signals, while positive feedback combined with quality portfolio samples indicate potential fit.

title: How A iPad Teleprompter Can Enhance Your Corporate Video
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
A teleprompter can be valuable for video production, functioning as a reading tool that consists of a screen or mirror and allows the talent to read a script while looking directly into the camera.

The primary advantages include allowing talent to focus on personality and eye contact rather than memorization. Transcendent Enterprise uses an iPad teleprompter controlled via the teleprompter+ 3 app, enabling remote script speed adjustments, font sizing, and background color modifications.

## Alternative Uses

Beyond traditional script reading, the iPad can serve as a video window for Zoom interactions, allowing audiences to see and engage with participants naturally. This approach reduces crew size by enabling remote script operation.

## When to Use Teleprompters

Teleprompters suit content requiring precise wording and factual accuracy. They work less effectively for emotionally-driven testimonials requiring empathy. I would highly recommend that the talent knows the script roughly and rehearses rather than going in blindly.

## Production Techniques

Best practices include:
- Multiple script rehearsals before recording
- Recording section-by-section rather than full scripts
- Maintaining large text positioned far from talent to prevent obvious eye movement
- Encouraging natural gestures and smiling

title: Why Promotional and Testimonial Video Is The Most Engaging Content
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
This Medium article discusses what makes promotional video content so engaging to consumers. Video marketing is effective at grabbing attention and retaining customers. That's why video is better than any other medium. It's also why Transcendent focuses on creating high quality professional video for top companies and brands.

In essence, video brings together two things that capture attention like nothing else: movement and noise. Both these features play a huge role in effective brand messaging. They allow companies to clearly communicate their goals and values. Unlike text, which relies on specific words, punctuation and visual cues (i.e. emojis) to set a desired tone.

Video marketing and promotional video also allow you to show instead tell. Customers want to see exactly what they are getting from companies. Dynamic visual storytelling creates a stronger connection between brand and the audience. It also helps build brand awareness. Testimonial style videos are especially helpful with forming a connection because they are more personable. Which would you trust more? A lengthy paragraph or a real person telling you their experience?

## Fast Facts about Video Production
- 87% of online marketers use video content
- 79% of all Internet traffic in 2018 was video
- A positive user experience with a video advertisement increases their chance of buying by 97%
- Users spend 88% more time on a website that has video

title: Top 3 Things To Remember When Preparing Yourself For A Live Zoom Broadcast Presentation
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
With the sudden boom of virtual events, many who are used to speaking in front of people live and in person are suddenly faced with the reality that they must now talk to a camera from their own homes.

How do you prepare yourself and living space for professional broadcast from home?

## 1. Best Way to Light Yourself for Zoom

Lighting establishes focus and mood. Record during daylight near windows with clean backgrounds. Wear contrasting colors against your backdrop. Maintain a single light source to prevent white balance issues that cause subjects to appear washed out, grey, or oversaturated. Incandescent lighting is preferred over fluorescent; ensure all bulbs match for consistency.

## 2. Best Way to Capture Sound for Zoom

Use headphones with attached microphones or standard phone earbuds. Select a quiet room eliminating background noise and white noise interference.

## 3. Best Way to Have Quality in Zoom

Maintain fastest internet speeds near your WiFi router (within 10 feet recommended). Check speeds via Google's speed test tool. Target minimums: 10 mbps download/5 mbps upload, with acceptable baseline of 5 mbps down/1 mbps up.

Following these three principles enables professional computer presentations matching in-person speaking quality while establishing appropriate mood alignment with content.

title: How To Set Up Real-Time Captioning On Live Videos
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
Everything you need to know about Live Captioning and ASL Interpreted for Live Stream Events

Closed captioning provides accessibility for videos to audiences worldwide. Live closed captioning helps make professional livestream events accessible to hearing-impaired viewers.

With social media platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube increasingly featuring automatic live captioning, content creators can now reach wider audiences. This applies to both live streams and pre-recorded content.

## Closed Captions vs Live Captions

Closed captions typically accompany pre-recorded content that has been transcribed and timed to audio for accuracy. Live captioning, by contrast, applies real-time captions to ongoing video broadcasts. These captions may experience slight delays or occasional inaccuracies due to technological limitations.

Real-time closed captioning proves valuable for virtual events including lectures, presentations, meetings, and speeches, ensuring hearing-impaired participants can fully engage.

## ASL and Live Captions Through Zoom

In summer 2021, the organization produced a Zoom workshop for Lincoln Center featuring three cameras, closed captions, and an ASL interpreter in a single stream, ensuring comprehensive accessibility.

## Most Professional Live Stream Captions and Latest Update in 2022

### Falcon Encoder Approach
An encoder called Falcon transmits video signals to live captioners who overlay captions in real-time before distribution to audiences. This represents the traditional captioning methodology.

CaptioningStar provides user support for this process. Based on experience with Falcon through CaptioningStar, using dedicated recommended players is advisable. The organization previously tested Dacast, which failed to display for end-users, likely due to authentication requirements. Expect approximately $70 in CaptioningStar fees for Falcon demo access.

## Embedding Captions to a Web Page

Streamtext.net employs stenographers who transcribe audio to text during live streams. Video can be embedded into web pages with live caption displays.

Customization Features: Text size, font, color, and theme are adjustable. Output supports Quicktime, Windows Media, Real Player, or YouTube. Multiple caption language sets can be created for diverse audiences.

Important Considerations:
- Non-embedded streams require sending YouTube links and website URLs to the service
- Music selections must be provided in advance for lyrics captioning
- Live performances are captioned by stenographers in real-time
- The service operates only in English, potentially causing misspellings of non-English phrases
- One language per session; translations require feature activation

## Some Free Options For Live Captioning

"You get what you paid for." - Chris Rock

Free AI caption tools function adequately but operate within translation limitations.

### YouTube
YouTube Live creators can activate automatic captions using AI technology for normal latency streams.
Requirements:
1. Channel must have captions feature enabled (typically 10k+ subscribers)
2. Audio quality must be high
3. Speakers cannot overlap or quickly switch languages

### Webcaptioner.com (Best Option)
This platform embeds captions to live streams with AI transcription capabilities. OBS (free) or Wirecast encoders enable caption integration.

Advantages:
- Embeds to any player or encoder with screen capture capability
- Provides real-time translation of live captions
- Free service
- User-friendly interface for professionals
- No cost barrier

Disadvantages:
- Translation accuracy varies
- Audio routing to the web platform proves challenging

## Zoom Closed Captions for Videos

Zoom offers three captioning options: manual caption provision, third-party service integration, or Zoom's live transcription feature.

Current Limitations:
- English language support only
- Accuracy depends on background noise levels, speaker volume, and speech clarity (dialects and accents affect performance)

These methods enable live stream closed captioning across multiple platforms, expanding content accessibility.

title: Conferences 2022: Hosting An In Person Conference With Virtual Panelist
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
Despite feeling as such, it wasn't too long ago that people held conferences in large venues where hundreds of people would gather around to hear various people speak and answer questions. In order to have an event like this take place, someone would need to scout for locations, provide food, and most importantly: find potential panelists to participate in said event. With most conferences and other events being held virtually, it is even easier to invite others to speak on behalf of your company or organization. It is also important to understand the effect having these speakers can have on the success of your event.

At live events, everyone is based in one set location. This right off the bat may lead to the exclusion of some potential panelists speaking at the event. Unless you, or they themselves, are willing to pay for travel and hotel fare, it is most likely those who live in the nearby area who will be attending the event. Depending on how large an organization you are, this can harshly cut the speaker options you have to work with (as well as the number of attendees). By hosting an in-person conference with a virtual panelist you can have any number of speakers available from all around the world. This can lead to attendees interacting with leaders, managers, or speakers throughout the organization that they may never get a chance to network with normally.

This increase in potential panelists can also improve attendee participation. It is more likely that a person will ask questions or answer polls when speaking with someone they've never interacted with before, especially someone higher up in a company or who is too busy to travel across the country. Live events also have the added benefit of being recorded, so it can be uploaded and watched after the event is over. It has the ability to be reached by thousands of people, who may have never heard of you in the first place, and can lead to a larger audience for future events.

These potential new speakers can also lead to more interesting seminars. Having unique speakers can lead to discussions with multiple perspectives, good anecdotes, better problem solving, and demonstration in different techniques. The flexibility in time and location allows you to seek out higher quality speakers and offer them a schedule that is more convenient for them, something they may not have been able to do at an in-person event.

It is crucial that for events such as these, you hire a professional crew that can take care of the technical aspects of the event. An experienced company, such as ourselves, Transcendent Enterprise, will be able to set up the space of the event, enabling you to have rehearsals, check internet speed quality, and fix any hiccups in the process before the main event. The panelists and clients should see a feed of the event playing as if it is an edited video. They hear and see the camera as if they were there in a viewer link in zoom, but there will be the option to have power points, videos, and graphic movement as they are speaking. This company should also be coordinating the audio and AV equipment to set up the virtual panelists on screen, so that their sound is clear and in sync with the picture.

It is important to bring outside voices into these conversations to give viewers something to look forward to. Having a good panelist can lead to in depth conversations and excellent attendee feedback, while costing barely half as much as it would to hold an in-person event. Broaden your organization's horizons, and find new ways to keep audiences engaged!

## Hybrid Live Stream vs. Fully Virtual Graduation Ceremony

When it comes to planning a virtual graduation, there are two routes schools can take. The first is a hybrid live stream ceremony. Hybrid ceremonies are traditional graduations that are streamed online that can have virtual speakers or other multimedia elements. This option works well if most students and faculty can be there in person, but some family or staff are unable to travel.

Fully virtual ceremonies are becoming less common as we near the end of the COVID-19 pandemic. Virtual graduations take place completely online and are usually streamed on platforms like Zoom or Facebook so attendees can interact. It can be daunting for schools to plan their graduation considering how quickly live streaming software and technology has advanced. However, here are 3 tips that anyone can use to improve their virtual graduation ceremony in 2023.

## 1. Choose The Best Streaming Platform For Your School's Graduation Live Stream

There are dozens of different options for streaming your school's graduation online. It is possible to live stream on multiple platforms at once, but it's easiest to put time and effort into one stream to keep everything streamlined. Popular options for streaming virtual graduations in 2023 are YouTube and Zoom.

The best way to choose a platform is to consider what you want to include in your school's ceremony. Do you need closed captioning? Do you need to transition between live and virtual speakers? Are there pre-recorded videos or music that you want to play? Different platforms can accommodate different assets, so it's important to consider what you want to do before planning and scheduling your live stream virtual graduation.

## 2. Create Video Content For Your Virtual Graduation Ceremony

It can be challenging to include remote or unavailable students, faculty, and staff. One way to make them a part of the ceremony is to have them film pre-recorded videos that can be shown during the ceremony. Highlight reels and student performances are also other examples of video content that can be included to elevate virtual graduations and make them special for all attendees.

## 3. Look At What Has Made Other Virtual Graduations Successful

Virtual graduations have been going on since 2020. Some schools have live streamed their graduations even before that. ## What Is a Sizzle Reel?

More than just a cool phrase, a sizzle reel is a form of promotional video. There are some people or agencies who might refer to these short promotional videos by another name. For example, demo reels, pitch tapes, or highlight reels are other common references to the same media type. However, we feel that the term "sizzle" is the most accurate representation for this type of video marketing.

While there is not a single type of sizzle reel example, the model tends to operate with best practices in mind. First and foremost, sizzle reel videos tend to be short and flashy. This doesn't have to mean loud music and over-the-top special effects. Simply put, it means they are designed to grab attention quickly.

Because of this, they tend to be fast paced. And this pace tends to rely on video editing and filming techniques more than the product itself. It doesn't matter what type of product or service your business provides. You can take advantage of this type of promo video for whatever you do.

For instance, you might be introducing your business at a conference, and want a video that showcases what you do. Or you might want to highlight a client relationship or case study on social media.

Another example of a way to utilize this platform is for pitching new projects. For instance, you might have an idea for a TV show, and want to make the case for your content quickly. Introducing the elements of your proposed show in a snappy way can be more effective than simply reading a log line.

Sizzle reels are an excellent way to capture just about anything related to your project in a short and effective way.

## Why are Sizzle Reels Effective?

Everybody likes a quick-hitting video. For one thing, they are not long enough to lose attention spans. Because of their editing style and upbeat music, they pull people in.

For another thing, sizzle videos tend to use few words, either with on-screen text or narration. With so few words, the point is made quickly.

Additionally, since they are short and usually used on social media, they are shareable pieces of content. Easier than sharing a written case study and more engaging than sharing a blog post.

In a sense, think of a sizzle reel as a quick-hitting greatest hits of what you are trying to convey. From promoting a new product, to pitching a TV show, to spreading awareness about your company, to almost anything else. The bottom line is that sizzle reels are effective.

title: What is the Goal of a Testimonial?
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
## Understanding the Impact of Customer Feedback

There is no denying that consumers take peer-to-peer feedback seriously. Whether trying to make a purchasing decision or booking a restaurant reservation, positive reviews make a difference.

This is why testimonials are important. However, while client testimonials are a way to hear from real customers, they are not exactly the same as peer reviews. For instance, social media and review sites like Yelp are full of both negative and positive reviews. So what sets customer testimonials apart?

## The Importance of Client Testimonials

By definition, a testimonial is a formal statement testifying to someone's character or qualifications. This could pertain to either a product or service. And the formality of a customer testimonial is what makes it unique. It's also what sets the bar higher than a quote or thumbs up or a review site.

Sure, a review on social media could lead to potential customers. But when a customer is willing to show their face, or lend their voice to their review, it builds trust. And if there is one thing that any business wants to achieve, it is trust in its product or service.

Video testimonials bring reviews to life. They show real customer stories, which enables an audience to identify more easily. And what's more, because they come in the form of videos, they can be fun.

## Types of Testimonials

Customer testimonials can come in all shapes and sizes. This is because a video format can achieve just about any mood or style. For instance, you might want to have a seated interview with a client in their home. This "letting the audience in" provides a level of honesty to the review.

On the other hand, you might want to see a customer in action. Let's say you have a coffee shop, for example, and therefore you want to show customers in your setting. Hearing from your customers in your shop can demonstrate how comfortable everyone feels. It speaks not only to the quality of your coffee, but to the environment as well.

A video testimonial is important because it demonstrates the vibe of your business. It makes an audience want to be part of that vibe. And thanks to video production and editing, any type of vibe can be created.

## Video Makes a Difference

The goal of a testimonial is to reach your audience, and enable them to identify with your business. Nothing achieves this like video production. Videos are more fun and engaging than quoted reviews or selecting a number of stars. Additionally, customers are more likely to share a video than a review.

If you are looking to promote your business and engage with your audience, then it is worth considering a video testimonial. It is the best way to have the most powerful impact.

Reach out to learn more about our corporate video production services, and let's create a video that represents your business.

title: Behind the Scenes: Exploring the Role of a Production Company
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
When we think of the work that goes into movies or TV, we often focus on the actors first. But there's a whole host of other people involved in bringing these movies to life, and one crucial aspect of that is a production company.

But what does a production company do? And what role do they play in the finished product? Here, we'll dive into exactly what a film production company does and why they're so important.

## What Does a Media Production Company Do?

Production companies are at the heart of the film industry: They are responsible for literally producing the film, TV show, or other form of media. Basically, they run the show from start to finish, from developing the concept to doing market research and planning distribution.

Here are a few more things a video product company helps manage during the process:

### Developing Content
In today's world, it seems like we're all developing content, but a production company will help develop the main idea of the movie or TV show, as well as help produce social media content for promotion and distribution.

### Scripting
Once the idea is formed, it's time to get it down on paper. Production companies will help facilitate the scriptwriting process, whether that means hiring a writing team or using in-house writers.

### Casting & Hiring
Production companies will hire actors, directors, and all of the crew needed to create the video -- and that's a long list! If you've ever watched the credits of a movie roll you know just how many people are involved in the production process, so this is no small feat.

### Planning & Logistics
Just like there are a lot of people involved in making a video, there are a lot of logistics involved. Production companies will coordinate travel, filming on location, making sure there are craft services and trailers at those locations along with all other logistical aspects.

### Editing & Post-Production
The work doesn't end once you've filmed the video. There's editing and post-production work to be done, and the production company will handle this as well. They may work with other firms to make sure all the technical details are done right, while acting as the liaison between all parties.

### Marketing & Production Distribution
Okay, the video is done. Now it's time to work on production distribution and make sure that people actually get to see it. Earlier on in the process, the film production company would conduct market research to see if the movie or show is on the right track in terms of audience, intended marketing plan, and other aspects. Once the film is done, it's time to distribute it, using the information gained in the research phase.

## Learn more about video production services

It may sound like all the responsibilities of a production company only apply to blockbuster movies or TV, but that's not the case.

Every video, no matter how small, can benefit from the expertise of a production company. Learn more about Transcendent Enterprises' video services, and contact us to find out how we can help bring your video to life today.

title: Going Live: A Step-by-Step Guide to Livestreaming Your Event
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
Live event streaming has jumped in popularity over the past few years, but this kind of audience engagement is here to stay. Even without the constraints of the COVID pandemic, live streaming an event is a great way to make it more accessible to more people.

But how can you livestream an event? Here we'll give you a step-by-step guide to livestreaming your event, from set-up to follow-up.

## The Best Way to Livestream an Event: Step-by-Step

Before you get started livestreaming your event, there's some research and preparation that you have to do first. Let's dive in:

### Step 1: Identify Your Goals for the Event
Before you go live, you need to have a solid understanding of what you hope to achieve with your event. That way, you can tailor all your promotional materials, the event itself, and your follow-up actions to help you meet those goals.

### Step 2: Plan Your Event
Once you have an idea of what you want to achieve, you can actually get down to planning your live event streaming. This is where you start to plan things like who will be speaking, whether or not all panelists will be in their own homes or if you'll rent a shared space, and other logistical things.

This step is also a good place to make sure that you've researched live streaming platforms and chosen the one that makes sense for you. Think about the cost of the program, what kind of video specs it offers, and if there are monetization opportunities for you to take advantage of.

### Step 3: Day of Set Up
Arguably, the easiest way to livestream an event would be to have all the speakers in the same place. This way, you can set up all your video and audio recording equipment once for all the speakers, removing some of the opportunity for technical difficulties.

There are also tons of options for livestreaming an event even if the panelists are spread all across the country or the world. If you are going to have multiple people joining remotely, make sure you set up time ahead of the live broadcast to test out everyone's tech, lighting, and audio.

### Step 4: Stream Your Event
Now that you've done all the prep, you're ready to actually get into live event streaming. Make sure that all your speakers are set up and know what they need to do, your tech is all set, and you'll be ready to go live!

### Step 5: Follow Up with Your Audience
To bring your event full circle, think back to step one when you set your goals for the event. Use your goal from that set to guide how and when you follow up with your audience. Whatever action you wanted your audience to take, make sure you remind them with a follow up email.

## The Best Way to Livestream an Event

Live event streaming is a great way to increase your reach, make your events more accessible to more people, and provide value to your customers. To make the event as successful as possible, you want to go beyond a simple recorded webinar.

To learn more about how you can create an authentic event experience in a virtual world, reach out to Transcendent Enterprise today. We'll walk you through every step of setting up your virtual event.

title: Understanding Video Editing Costs: Factors, Estimates, and Budgeting Tips
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
Video editing requires significant time investment and professional expertise to master. If you possess raw video footage requiring editing, you may wonder about typical editor charges. The answer depends on multiple variables.

The pricing for video editing services varies based on video length, editing complexity, and whether the editor works independently or within a production company. This guide explores typical video editor rates and selecting the appropriate editor for your needs.

## Factors that Impact Video Editor Rates

Video editor rates range from approximately $50 to $150 hourly, depending on the project and professional. Several factors influence final pricing:

### Experience level
An editor's years of experience significantly impacts costs. Beginning editors typically charge $20-$45 hourly, while experienced professionals may charge $100-$150 hourly. Review potential editors' portfolios beforehand. Beginners might possess exactly what you need cost-effectively, but complex projects warrant investing in experienced professionals.

### Payment type
Editors invoice differently -- daily rates, hourly fees, or fixed project costs. Each model affects final expenses. Confirm payment preferences and rates align with your budget before contracting.

### Freelance or full-time
Independent freelancers set their own rates with flexible pricing options, while full-time production house employees have company-set rates. However, freelancers may lack resources, tools, and team support that full-time employees access. Production teams offer comprehensive experiences despite potentially higher costs.

title: Dress to Impress: A Guide to What to Wear on Camera for Your Next Video Shoot
author: Transcendent Enterprise
content:
## Best Clothes to Wear on Camera

When selecting an outfit for video, clothing choice varies based on the video's purpose and intended message. Interview clothing guidelines generally apply here as well. Garments should be clean, wrinkle-free, and age-appropriate. Unless the video focuses on fashion or design, traditional clothing like suits, solid colors, and collared shirts work best. Ultimately, apparel should reinforce your message rather than divert attention from it.

## Best Colors to Wear on Camera

Clothing color matters significantly, as it may appear differently on video and could distract from your message. Solid colors perform best; large prints prove distracting and colors may blend together. Bright whites and bright reds should be avoided, as they transform noticeably on video and alter the overall tone.

Warm neutrals including browns, maroons, and blues are preferable choices. Green requires caution, especially if green screen usage is planned. Bringing multiple outfit options ensures backup if accidents occur or if one color doesn't photograph well.

While clothing significantly impacts the final product, camera setup plays an equally important role. Explore production studio setup options at Transcendent Enterprise.

title: Use Corporate Video to Change the Culture in your Company
author: chris@t-enter.com
content:
## Transforming Company Culture Through Storytelling and Laughter

When most people think about changing company culture, they picture team workshops, HR policies, or maybe a new mission statement. But at Transcendent Enterprise, we've discovered a different way to spark connection, boost morale, and make employees genuinely feel like they belong -- through storytelling and a little bit of comedy.

Here's how we're helping corporations shift their culture, one creative video at a time.

## Humanizing the Brand with Employee Profile Videos

One of the most successful projects involved working with Facebook to create employee profile videos. These weren't stiff, corporate talking heads. They were real stories -- day-in-the-life moments that captured the human side of people working behind the scenes.

These videos serve multiple purposes:
- Boost internal pride by spotlighting unsung heroes
- Improve onboarding by showcasing authentic experiences
- Strengthen external brand by showing prospective hires what culture really looks like

When employees see themselves reflected in the company narrative, it changes how they show up to work. They're not just a cog in the machine -- they're a visible, valued part of the story.

## Lifting Spirits with SNL-Style Skits

Now here's where things get fun.

Another project involved producing Saturday Night Live-style comedy skits written by and starring the employees themselves. That's right -- your finance guy might just become the next weekend update star (for one day).

These skits were more than a gimmick. They gave employees a chance to:
- Collaborate across departments
- Let off steam in a high-energy, creative environment
- Celebrate the quirks of company culture with humor and humility

The team worked closely with an internal point of contact who provided a rough script outline based on the company's inside jokes, values, or current events. Then production support followed -- lights, cameras, laughs.

The results? Skyrocketing morale, deepened team bonds, and a viral-worthy video employees couldn't stop sharing.

## Why It Works: Connection > Compensation

In an era where burnout is real and disengagement is high, what teams are really craving isn't another free lunch or ping-pong table. It's connection.

When companies invest in projects that allow employees to:
- Be seen
- Be heard
- Be themselves

...it sparks a cultural shift that no memo ever could.

Whether it's a beautifully shot profile series or a low-stakes day of improv in front of the camera, these experiences create memories, build trust, and reinforce a sense of shared purpose.

## Final Thoughts: Culture is a Camera Away

If your organization is struggling to unify remote teams, energize your workforce, or reestablish pride after years of change -- consider storytelling as your tool.

Because at the end of the day, culture isn't what you say it is.
It's what your people feel.
And we're here to help them feel seen.
